description: Special rules defined in the Alternate 40k Core Rule Book. These are referenced by id in faction files. Faction-specific rules are defined in their own faction file. Unit-unique rules are defined in unit.inlineRules.

rules.name: AA
rules.fullDesc: Weapons with AA ignore the –1 To Hit against High Altitude models and gain +1 To Hit against Flyers and High Altitude models. They also ignore the Cover Save models receive from Deepstriking.

rules.name: Accurate
rules.fullDesc: This weapon has +1 To Hit.

rules.name: Aquatic
rules.fullDesc: This model ignores the Difficult Terrain penalty for moving through Water Terrain as well as the risk of getting stuck.

rules.name: Armourbane
rules.fullDesc: Roll 2d6 to penetrate Vehicles and Titanic Vehicles instead of 1d6.

rules.name: Assault Ramp
rules.fullDesc: Models Disembarking from a Transport with Assault Ramps may Declare Charges on the same turn they Disembark.

rules.name: Aura
rules.fullDesc: Auras usually affect the user model and a target unit, but will specify in the description. One unit cannot benefit from Auras of the same name at the same time, but may still benefit from Auras of different names with the same effect. Your Auras end and may be cast again in your Command Phase.

rules.name: Auto Hit
rules.fullDesc: An Auto Hit always succeeds in hitting the target.

rules.name: Auto Wound
rules.fullDesc: An Auto Wound is an automatic successful Wound that has AP–. Units still get their full Armour and Invulnerability Saves as well as Feel No Pain, but do not get Cover Saves from Auto Wounds.

rules.name: Assault #
rules.fullDesc: Assault weapons can charge in the same turn they shoot. They no longer get to fire after advancing.

rules.name: Auxiliary
rules.fullDesc: Auxiliary weapons can never have any Base Attacks assigned to them. They operate like an additional Melee Weapon with one Attack that may receive more from the Extra Attack rule only.

rules.name: Beam
rules.fullDesc: Select a target model (owning player still determines which models are hit) and roll To Hit as normal. For each model in between the Beam weapon and the target in a 1" wide path, roll a d6. On each 5+, that model is Hit just like with a template.

rules.name: Bodyguard
rules.fullDesc: A model with Bodyguard may attempt to stop a Wound, Glance, or Penetration that gets through a Character or Independent Character's Saves and Feel No Pains. For each Wound the Character or Independent Character would have suffered, the Bodyguard suffers that many Mortal Wounds on a roll of a 2+. On a 1, the Character or Independent Character suffers the Wounds like normal.

rules.name: Bomb
rules.fullDesc: To use a Bomb, select a target model you moved over in the Movement Phase. Apply the Blast or Flame Thrower Template and roll the scatter dice. Bomb Scattering works just like Blast weapons, where your BS score reduces the amount of inches it moves on the 2d6. A Direct Hit never scatters.

rules.name: Building
rules.fullDesc: This model is a fortification Building. It cannot move. Units may embark inside it and fire from its Fire Ports as normal.

rules.name: Bulky
rules.fullDesc: This model takes two Transport spaces. This includes Firing Ports when manning them.

rules.name: Character
rules.fullDesc: Characters may issue and accept Challenges. Characters also may choose which model is hit when they roll a natural 6 To Hit for both Ranged and Melee attacks.

rules.name: Co-Axial
rules.fullDesc: Co-Axial weapons must be fired at the same target. Select one weapon to shoot. If it scores at least 1 hit against the target, the second weapon may reroll misses or scatter dice.

rules.name: Combat Walker
rules.fullDesc: Combat Walkers always use their FA for defence when in Melee unless their M value has been reduced to 0. When at M0, all Melee attacks are considered to strike its RA regardless of facing.

rules.name: Concussion
rules.fullDesc: If a Concussive weapon wounds the target, it suffers A–1 for each one until the next Melee Phase of the Player's Turn who inflicted the Concussion. A model may never be reduced to less than A1.

rules.name: Counterattack
rules.fullDesc: This model is considered Charging when it is Charged. This can trigger other rules like Shock Assault and Furious Charge even though it did not Declare a Charge.

rules.name: Dangerous Terrain Test
rules.fullDesc: For each model in the unit roll a d6. On a 1, the model suffers a Mortal Wound, consolidating Wounds if necessary for multi-Wound models.

rules.name: Deepstrike
rules.fullDesc: Place the model with the highest leadership on the table and roll the scatter dice. Place all additional models in the unit around in base contact forming rings. If any model overlaps impassable terrain or another model, move the unit the minimum distance necessary; the entire unit is forced to Go to Ground and become Pinned. Models cannot Charge on a Turn when they arrived by Deepstrike. Models that Deepstrike gain a 4+ Cover Save until their next Movement Phase.

rules.name: Destroyer
rules.fullDesc: A Destroyer weapon always Wounds and Penetrates on a 2+. It will never Glance. Wounds and Penetrations from a Destroyer weapon ignore Armour Saves, Cover Saves, Invulnerability Saves, and Feel No Pain. Titanic targets suffer +2 Damage for each Destroyer Wound inflicted.

rules.name: Extra Attack #
rules.fullDesc: This weapon or ability grants # additional Attacks in Melee.

rules.name: Fearless
rules.fullDesc: Fearless units automatically pass Fear tests, Pinning Tests, and do not fall back when losing the combat round. If they fail Combat Resolution, for each point they fail the Ld test they suffer one Auto Wound.

rules.name: Feel No Pain
rules.fullDesc: This test is taken after Saves are taken on a unit. Like a Save, it succeeds on the number printed or higher. Feel No Pain does not apply vs Destroyer weapons, and cannot be granted to a Vehicle unit from an Aura source unless specified.

rules.name: Fleet
rules.fullDesc: This model rolls 3d6 choosing the highest d6 when Advancing.

rules.name: Flying
rules.fullDesc: This model may ignore Terrain, vertical distance, and models when Moving, Advancing, Charging, and Falling Back, but must end its movement in a space it can legally occupy.

rules.name: Furious Charge
rules.fullDesc: This model gains S+1 on a phase it is considered to have Charged.

rules.name: Gauss
rules.fullDesc: A Gauss weapon inflicts an Auto Wound on a natural 6 To Hit. Continue rolling to Wound or Penetrate as normal with the remaining dice in the pool.

rules.name: Gets Hot!
rules.fullDesc: When rolling To Hit, all natural 1s cause 1 Mortal Wound to the bearer of the weapon. If using templates, roll a d6 for each template placed and suffer a Mortal Wound for each 1 rolled. Continue rolling To Wound or Penetrate like normal.

rules.name: Grav
rules.fullDesc: Grav weapons always wound Infantry, Monstrous Infantry, Monsters, Swarms, and Titanic Monsters on a 4+. Buildings, Vehicles and Titanic Vehicles are Glanced on a 6+. Every Hit from a Grav weapon causes all Movement, Advances, Charges, and Fleeing to be reduced by 1" for each Hit until the same Player's Phase next Turn.

rules.name: Grenade #
rules.fullDesc: One grenade of each weapon equipped may be thrown by a unit during the shooting phase by a model instead of that model using its weapons. Allows the unit to ignore the penalty for charging a unit in cover if at least one model in the unit is equipped with Grenades. Non-Blast Grenades can be used in place of all melee weapons by trading all attacks for a single attack; multiple models can use the same grenade in Melee. Auxiliary Weapons cannot be used with Grenades by the same model in Melee.

rules.name: Haywire
rules.fullDesc: Haywire will always Glance a Vehicle or Titanic Vehicle on a #+. A natural roll of a 6 to Penetrate, regardless of the Armour Value, is always considered a Penetration.

rules.name: Heal
rules.fullDesc: A model or unit with the Heal ability restores 1 Wound during their Command Phase, up to their starting Wound count. A model cannot benefit from Healing more than once per turn.

rules.name: Heavy #
rules.fullDesc: Heavy weapons can only fire Snap Shots if they move on the turn they fire unless they are mounted to Vehicles, Monsters, Monstrous Infantry, or Titanic variants.

rules.name: High Altitude
rules.fullDesc: This model inflicts a –1 penalty To Hit from attacks that are not AA or from an Interceptor model. This model must move a minimum of 20" in each of its Movement Phases, and pivots up to 90 degrees at the end of each Movement. It is allowed to fly off the table and re-enter from Reserves.

rules.name: Hit and Run
rules.fullDesc: A model or unit with this ability may attempt to Hit and Run in their Movement Phase. Roll 1d6 and add the highest Initiative value of models with Hit and Run, and compare it to a roll of the highest enemy Initiative + d6. If equal or higher, the Hit and Run models may leave Melee and Move like normal.

rules.name: Hover
rules.fullDesc: This model may ignore its High Altitude Rule and reduce its maximum Movement Value to 20. It may not select to go back into High Altitude mode until its next Movement Phase.

rules.name: Hull
rules.fullDesc: This weapon is considered to have a 90 degree firing arc.

rules.name: Ignores Cover
rules.fullDesc: The attacks made by this weapon ignore any Cover Saves for any unit this weapon hits.

rules.name: Immobile
rules.fullDesc: This model cannot Move, Advance, Charge, or Fall Back under any circumstances.

rules.name: Independent Character
rules.fullDesc: Characters may issue and accept Challenges. Independent Characters may join units of the same model type. Hits against the joined unit are resolved at the unit's Toughness or Armour value. Independent Characters may attempt to save against a number of Wounds equal to their current Wound value per Phase. There must be at least 2 models in the base unit per Independent Character joined.

rules.name: Indirect
rules.fullDesc: An Indirect Fire weapon may fire at units it does not have line of sight to. An indirect fire weapon adds its BS to the amount scattered on the 2d6 for Blast weapons if the model does not have Line of Sight to the target. If the model can draw Line of Sight, the Indirect penalties do not apply.

rules.name: Infiltrate
rules.fullDesc: After deployment, you may place Infiltrators. Starting with the Player who is going first, place one Infiltrator unit, then the second Player may do likewise. Continue alternating until all Infiltrators are placed. They must be placed at least 12" from enemy models.

rules.name: Interceptor
rules.fullDesc: All weapons on this model ignore the –1 To Hit against High Altitude models, gain +1 To Hit against Flyers and High Altitude, and ignore the Cover Save units gain from Deepstriking. When an Interceptor Advances it may move up to its full M value instead of 1d6".

rules.name: Jump
rules.fullDesc: A model with Jump may choose to Jump during any form of movement, including Advancing, Charging, and Falling Back. If they do so, they count as Flying until their next Movement Phase.

rules.name: Lance
rules.fullDesc: This weapon treats opposing Armour Values as being no higher than 12 when rolling To Penetrate. Armour Values less than 12 remain at their Value.

rules.name: Linked
rules.fullDesc: Linked weapons must all target the same unit when firing.

rules.name: Melta
rules.fullDesc: When in half range, roll 2d6 for penetrating a Vehicle or Titanic Vehicle instead of 1d6. When in half range, Melta weapons have Monsterbane.

rules.name: Monsterbane
rules.fullDesc: A Monsterbane weapon inflicts +1 wound when successfully wounding a Monster or Building after saves and Feel No Pains (or Titanic Monsters). Against Monstrous Infantry, the user must reroll failed To Wound rolls instead.

rules.name: Nonblast
rules.fullDesc: This Grenade does not use a Blast template. It may be used in Melee in place of all Melee attacks, making a single attack instead.

rules.name: Objective Secured
rules.fullDesc: A model always claims an Objective Marker if it has this rule, even if it normally cannot claim Objectives or is outnumbered. If opposing units have Objective Secured, the side with the most models within range and with Objective Secured controls it.

rules.name: One Use Only
rules.fullDesc: Once used, the weapon or ability is expended and cannot be used again this game.

rules.name: Ordnance
rules.fullDesc: In addition to having the Pinning rule, when rolling to Penetrate Vehicles or Titanic Vehicles you roll 2d6 and pick the highest d6 result. The model cannot move and fire with Ordnance weapons unless it is Titanic.

rules.name: Outflank
rules.fullDesc: Models with Outflank may be held in Reserve. After turn one, at the end of any of your Movement Phases, you may deploy them. Roll a d6: 1–2 left table edge, 3–4 middle enemy deployment edge, 5–6 right table edge. Models arriving by Outflank may not Declare Charges the turn they arrive.

rules.name: Pinning
rules.fullDesc: Perform a Leadership test by rolling 2d6 minus the number of Wounds suffered by a Pinning weapon. If the test fails, the entire unit is Pinned. Pinned units gain +1 Cover Save or 6+ in the open, cannot move, may only fire Snap Shots, and cannot declare Charges or Cast spells. At the end of your next Movement Phase, roll a Ld test to rally; if passed they are no longer Pinned.

rules.name: Pistol #
rules.fullDesc: Pistols can be fired in close combat in your Shooting Phase and count as Assault weapons. These shots must target the unit they are engaged with. Pistols do not suffer the –1 To Hit when fired in Melee. Pistols Overwatch on a 5+ instead of the usual 6+.

rules.name: Poisoned
rules.fullDesc: Poisoned weapons always wound Infantry, Monstrous Infantry, Monsters, and Titanic Monsters on a #+. They only ever wound or Glance a Building or Vehicle and Titanic Vehicle on a 6+.

rules.name: Primary
rules.fullDesc: This weapon treats the secondary effects of Weapon Destroyed as Weapon Disabled. It still suffers the +2 Wounds from the Penetration Chart instead of +1.

rules.name: Psychic Bane
rules.fullDesc: Wounds caused by this weapon count double for the purposes of Combat Resolution.

rules.name: Psychic Disruption
rules.fullDesc: Units Hit by a Psychic Disruption weapon lose one ongoing Spell effect of the Defender's choosing.

rules.name: Psychic Mastery
rules.fullDesc: This model may cast a number of psychic powers per Phase equal to its Psychic Mastery Level. Higher levels allow access to more powerful powers.

rules.name: Rapid Fire #
rules.fullDesc: Rapid Fire weapons double the number of attacks they get when they are within half their range.

rules.name: Rending
rules.fullDesc: On a natural 5+ To Wound or Glance, this attack ignores the Armour Save of the target. If a 5 would not Wound or Glance the target normally, it does nothing.

rules.name: Repair
rules.fullDesc: There are two generic repair abilities. One restores 1d3 Wounds to a target Vehicle. The other restores 1 Wound and on a 5+ may restore a destroyed weapon, remove a weapon disable, step mobility back up, or remove a Weapon Disabled result. A target may only receive one Wound-restoring Repair per turn.

rules.name: Scatter
rules.fullDesc: If a Scatter weapon hits a target within half of its maximum range, add S+1 to the weapon profile for resolving the To Wound roll. Not to be confused with Blast Weapon scatter.

rules.name: Scout
rules.fullDesc: Units with Scout may move up to 12" after units and Infiltrators are deployed, but before the first turn. The Player winning Initiative moves all of their Scouts, followed by the second Player's Scouts.

rules.name: Shock Assault
rules.fullDesc: This model gains A+1 on the phase it is considered Charging.

rules.name: Shroud
rules.fullDesc: This model gains +1 to Cover Saves. If the model is in the open, it is always considered to have a 6+ Cover Save. If a unit Goes to Ground or is Pinned, they gain an additional +1 to a maximum of a 3+ Cover Save.

rules.name: Slow
rules.fullDesc: This weapon is considered to be Initiative 0 regardless of the bearer's Initiative value, even if it is considered to have Charged this phase. Other weapons used by this model fight at their normal Initiative speed.

rules.name: Sniper
rules.fullDesc: Sniper weapons may select the model targeted by the attacks. Snipers automatically wound Infantry, Monstrous Infantry, Monsters, Titanic Monsters, and Swarms on a #+, and are considered Rending weapons. Wounds caused by Snipers also cause Pinning Tests.

rules.name: Sponson
rules.fullDesc: This weapon is considered to have a 90 to 180 degree firing arc depending on the model.

rules.name: Steady
rules.fullDesc: This model does not count as having fired for the purposes of Declaring Charges but cannot exceed 8" in the distance Charged regardless of modifiers.

rules.name: Stealth
rules.fullDesc: This model gains +1 to its Cover Save to a maximum of 3+.

rules.name: Steed
rules.fullDesc: This model may not Embark on Transports and must roll a Dangerous Terrain Test when moving through a Terrain Feature that is not open like a Hill or Road.

rules.name: Stubborn
rules.fullDesc: This model ignores any Ld penalties from Combat Resolution when taking Morale tests.

rules.name: Tesla
rules.fullDesc: When rolling To Hit, a natural 6 generates 2 additional hits that are added to the dice pool to wound the target.

rules.name: Titanic
rules.fullDesc: Titanic units destroy all non-Titanic Terrain or non-Titanic Vehicle Wrecks they move into. They may leave combat if they can legally move. They always are considered sitting still for the purposes of firing weapons, and do not suffer the –1 To Hit or +1 To Scatter for template weapons when firing while engaged in Melee.

rules.name: Transport
rules.fullDesc: Models may Embark or Disembark in the Movement Phase by moving into base contact or from designated exit points. Infantry take up one space, Bulky models take two, Very Bulky take three. If destroyed, all Embarked models Force Disembark and are Pinned.

rules.name: Turret
rules.fullDesc: This weapon is considered to have a 270 to 360 degree arc of fire depending on the model.

rules.name: Very Bulky
rules.fullDesc: This model takes up 3 Transport spaces. This includes Firing Ports when manning them.

rules.name: Void Shields
rules.fullDesc: Models with this rule treat all Armour Facings as 12 against ranged attacks. Each time a ranged attack Penetrates this Armour Facing, ignore the result and remove one Shield. Once all Shields have fallen, use normal Armour Facings. Against ranged attacks that Penetrate once Void Shields have fallen, this model has a 5+ Invulnerability Save. Against all ranged attacks that Glance, even if Shields have fallen, this model has a 5+ Invulnerability Save.

rules.name: Wreckage
rules.fullDesc: A Vehicle or Titanic Vehicle that becomes Wrecked remains as a new piece of terrain. It becomes T7 terrain with a Wound count equal to its original Wound count. Excess Wounds that caused its destruction are lost. Wreckage of unspecified origins has W10 for normal Vehicles and W20 for Titanic.

rules.name: Bolter
rules.fullDesc: This weapon is a Bolter-type weapon and interacts with faction rules that reference the Bolter keyword, such as Bolter Discipline and Malicious Volleys.

rules.name: Swarm
rules.fullDesc: When a Monstrous Infantry, Monster, or Vehicle model is in base contact with a Swarm in Close Combat, it may not fire any weapons to targets outside of its melee except Flame Throwers. Swarms cannot Clog the Barrels of Titanic models. Swarms take 1 extra wound when wounded by Blast or Flame weapons.

rules.name: Infantry
rules.fullDesc: Infantry models are the only type that can claim or Contest Objective Markers. Infantry maintain 2" coherency between models.

rules.name: Monstrous Infantry
rules.fullDesc: Monstrous Infantry can fire any weapon while in Melee with a –1 To Hit and +1 To Scatter distances. They may even fire out of Melee. Monsterbane weapons must reroll failed To Wound rolls against Monstrous Infantry.

rules.name: Monster
rules.fullDesc: Monsters boast high Toughness and good melee. Monsterbane inflicts +1 extra wound when they fail saves. Monsters can fire any weapon in Melee with –1 To Hit and +1 To Scatter. Monsters may move through Occupiable Terrain, trading Auto Hits with it.

rules.name: Vehicle
rules.fullDesc: Vehicles use Front, Side, and Rear Armour Facings instead of Toughness. To Penetrate, roll d6 + weapon Strength against the relevant Armour Facing. A tie or a natural 6 causes a Glancing Hit; exceeding the value causes a Penetrating Hit with a Vehicle Damage Chart result.

rules.name: Fear
rules.fullDesc: Units in Melee with a model that has Fear must pass a Leadership test at the start of the Melee phase or suffer penalties as defined by the Fear-causing model's rules.

rules.name: We Shall Know No Fear
rules.fullDesc: Models with We Shall Know No Fear automatically pass Ld tests for Rallying and do not test for fleeing allies.

rules.name: Tank
rules.fullDesc: This model is a Tank. It may perform Tank Shocks and Ram attacks.

rules.name: Open Topped
rules.fullDesc: Models embarked on this vehicle may fire their weapons. However, Blast and Template weapons that hit the vehicle also roll to wound/penetrate against each embarked model.

rules.name: Rampage
rules.fullDesc: This model gains A+d3 if outnumbered in Melee.

rules.name: Two-Handed
rules.fullDesc: A model armed with this weapon cannot use any other melee weapon in the same Fight phase.

rules.name: Unique
rules.fullDesc: This unit may only appear once in your army.
